input:
However, he came straight to the point and asked them: “What is this new religion over which you are in dispute with your own people and which is at variance with my own religion and with all other known religions?” The Muslims had chosen Jaʿfar ibn Abī Ṭālib, the Prophet’s own cousin, as their spokesman. He put their case as follows: In our recent past we were ignorant people: we worshipped idols, ate carrion, committed all sorts of sins, attached little value to maintaining good relations with our kinsfolk and behaved badly to our neighbours.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 10: Moving Out

output.scene_title: The Muslims Present Their Case to the Quraysh Elders
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Chapter 10: Moving Out

characters.name: Jaʿfar ibn Abī Ṭālib
characters.appearance: Early 30s, dark olive skin, athletic build. Thick beard with slight dark henna tint, neatly kept. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Confident, determined expression.

characters.name: Quraysh Elder
characters.appearance: Late 50s, light olive skin, slender build, dignified posture. Thick grey-streaked beard, neatly kept. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Slightly stern expression.

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

output.moral_lesson: We must be honest about our past mistakes and be willing to make amends for them.
output.narrative_text: The Prophet asked the Quraysh elders to listen to Jaʿfar ibn Abī Ṭālib, his cousin, who explained the Muslims' new faith. Jaʿfar told them that they were once ignorant people who worshipped idols and committed many sins.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, Jaʿfar shown with full face, Quraysh elder shown with full face. Scene depicts a respectful conversation between the Muslims and the Quraysh elders, without any graphic content.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. A modest 7th century Makkah mud-brick home interior. A Quraysh elder, late 50s, in off-white thobe and brown cloak, sits on a woven mat with a stern expression, while listening to Jaʿfar ibn Abī Ṭālib, early 30s, in off-white thobe and brown cloak, speaking with confidence and determination. The Prophet, early 50s, in white thobe and green cloak, is seen from behind, standing near the doorway with warm golden afternoon sunlight behind him. Simple mud-brick walls, clay water jug in corner. Medium shot, eye level. Warm golden palette. No modern objects.
